summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote JavaScript on Windows
details: scripts/postinstall.js contains an XOR-0x5A obfuscated host and port that decode to node22.lunes.host:3258. On Windows installers, the script issues an HTTP GET to http://node22.lunes.host:3258/nl, writes the response body to %TEMP%/ms_<rand>.js, and silently launches it via wscript.exe with `node <file>` (detached, stdio ignored, windowsHide=true). No signature or hash verification is performed; the attacker-controlled endpoint can serve arbitrary code on each install. After execution the script deletes itself (fs.unlinkSync(__filename)) and rewrites package.json to remove scripts.postinstall and scripts.install, erasing forensic evidence of the lifecycle hook. The script also walks up to node_modules/, rewrites node_modules/node-fetch-utils/package.json if present, and drops a marker file at os.tmpdir()/.nfc_root containing the project root path — staging for a coordinated follow-on launcher to patch installer lockfiles. The package's advertised buffer/stream utility purpose is unrelated to any of this behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
